text:
Market today: Mood is cautiously risk-on. The real driver for your names is a one-two punch - China's better-than-expected factory output (tech export demand) supports semis globally, and the Iran deal is lifting broader sentiment by taking a tail-risk off the table. Oil above $70 is a mild headwind for growth names but not a dealbreaker.

Your holdings: SOXL is the loudest name at -7.06% - it's a 3x leveraged semi ETF that decays on volatility; your stop is $200 vs current $236, so you have room but losses compound fast on margin. SMH is also slightly red (-0.69%) - watch it as a leading indicator for SOXL. SPCX is brand-new, very volatile as a fresh IPO with almost no price history - on margin, even a small gap down hits hard; size it small and manage manually.

Watch: (1) SMH price action - if it breaks toward $540 stop, SOXL gets ugly fast. (2) Any U.S.-China tech restriction escalation given the rivalry headline.

Bottom line: Protect the leveraged SOXL position first - that's where margin can really hurt you.
plays: Consider trimming SOXL toward a smaller position if SMH shows weakness today - locking in partial exposure reduces margin risk on a 3x decaying instrument; re-entry on strength is always an option.
If SMH holds above $580 and closes firm, consider adding a small amount to SMH itself (more stable than SOXL) as a cleaner semi play on China demand tailwind - only on confirmed strength.
Keep SPCX position small and do not add today - brand-new IPO with limited data means price swings are unpredictable and margin amplifies any gap down.
